one_time_description: Government of Canada settlement received in 2023 (no prior year comparative)
economic_notes: Flying Dust has business enterprises in Construction Services, Real Estate Services, Energy Services, and Retail Services. First Nation has guaranteed $15,238,382 of Nation business entity debt. Investment in Gensource Potash Corporation held at fair value. Flying Dust Flare Gas Limited Partnership is a GBE. Member loan program exists.
notes: Fiscal year ends March 31, 2023 — this is the 2023 fiscal year, not 2024. Province is Saskatchewan (SK). Four entities fully consolidated (Flying Dust First Nation, CMHC Housing Program, Housing Corporation, Community Development Fund, Security Inc. = 5 entities including band itself). Nine GBEs accounted for under modified equity method. New accounting policy adopted in 2023 for asset retirement obligations (PS 3280) resulting in $1,807,000 ARO liability and $1,644,000 increase in TCA. Government of Canada settlement of $1,207,267 is a one-time non-recurring item. Total government transfers calculated as ISC + MLHSDA + MLTC CFS + MLTC Program + CMHC + Province of SK + FNLM Resource + MLTC RDI + Northwest School Division + Government of Canada settlement = 14115305+2810774+1186163+790731+457581+148717+76967+1477778+606841+1207267 = 22878124; OSR = 29689072 - 22878124 = 6810948. Remuneration schedule not included in provided text. No schedule of salaries/wages by object fully provided.
